title: The first encounter with Rei
wikitext:
[]: The first encounter with Rei             Quote Post by FrostyS » Fri Jul 03, 2009 10:40 am Forgive me if this is a repost but I'm incredibly curious about everyone's take on the first shot of Rei in episode one and it's signifigance with the story in general. Also note that the exact same scene occurs at the end of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Fri Jul 03, 2009 12:07 pm It was for style points.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Fri Jul 03, 2009 12:26 pm There's not a clear cut answer, really. Take a look at these threads for some theories and fanwankery. http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=2942 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6837 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=710

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 03, 2009 12:53 pm Rei's soul is made of quantum magic and can travel through time, basically.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Jul 03, 2009 1:35 pm Three words: Temporal causality loop. In Evangelion, there is no other way for events to proceed other than how they did. Anno uses EoE and EoTV to literally break down the 4th wall and remind us all that we're watching a story unfold, and that these characters are only real because we choose to believe in their existence. Thus Rei's ghostly apparition is there for thematic symmetry as well as a sort of echo from the end backwards toward the beginning. I guess it doesn't make a whole lot of sense if you don't already know what I'm trying to say. As far as symmetry goes, you see similar things going on with Asuka's eye patch & arm cast in EoE compared to Rei's eye patch & arm cast in Ep. 01--and even with Unit-01 getting its eye shot out and arm snapped in the same episode.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 03, 2009 3:45 pm Merridian wrote: Three words: Temporal causality loop.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Fri Jul 03, 2009 8:57 pm Merridian wrote: Three words: Temporal causality loop. In Evangelion, there is no other way for events to proceed other than how they did. Anno uses EoE and EoTV to literally break down the 4th wall and remind us all that we're watching a story unfold, and that these characters are only real because we choose to believe in their existence. Thus Rei's ghostly apparition is there for thematic symmetry as well as a sort of echo from the end backwards toward the beginning. I guess it doesn't make a whole lot of sense if you don't already know what I'm trying to say. As far as symmetry goes, you see similar things going on with Asuka's eye patch & arm cast in EoE compared to Rei's eye patch & arm cast in Ep. 01--and even with Unit-01 getting its eye shot out and arm snapped in the same episode.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jul 03, 2009 9:11 pm schismatics wrote: Merridian wrote: Three words: Temporal causality loop. In Evangelion, there is no other way for events to proceed other than how they did. Anno uses EoE and EoTV to literally break down the 4th wall and remind us all that we're watching a story unfold, and that these characters are only real because we choose to believe in their existence. Thus Rei's ghostly apparition is there for thematic symmetry as well as a sort of echo from the end backwards toward the beginning. I guess it doesn't make a whole lot of sense if you don't already know what I'm trying to say.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Fri Jul 03, 2009 9:45 pm @Merridian: What is the theme that's being echoed? @Nemz: If the Rei in ep01 was indeed the one from GNR, and it wouldn't be there if Rei didn't become GNR the way she did, then this "temporal causality loop" is certainly one of the better explanations for its occurrence. However, I agree that it's not fact as it stands, and personally, I also don't think it's correct. @FrostyS: Unfortunately, "I have no bloody clue."

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jul 04, 2009 2:53 pm Third Impact was inevitable, so it's a proper time loop and Merridian is 100% right.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sat Jul 04, 2009 3:53 pm @schismatics: Yeah, I know. If you think that's insane, check out Thomas Pynchon-- Gravity's Rainbow is actually what gave me the idea. That book is so cool it makes my head explode. Onwards. NemZ wrote: I think what you meant to say is that Rei has transcended the limitations of linear time at the end and thus is free to observe the lives of Shinji or others from whatever vantage point she chooses...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jul 14, 2009 3:59 am Merridian wrote: Since I've done my best to discount the easy answers, my only remaining theory was the time-loop.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Wed Jul 15, 2009 1:53 pm NemZ wrote: It's entirely consistent to say that ep1 phantom Rei was from a world were 3rd impact happened but that doesn't mean this is the same world she came from.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 15, 2009 3:44 pm Not sure why it wasn't linked to yet, but this page seems to explain things well: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Rei%27s_Ghostly_Appearances_and_Quantum_Mechanics Rei in ep1 and Rei in EoE ARE one in the same, it's also the Rei that Shinji sees in the train car visions which just so happens to be Rei 3. Lilith took on Rei 3's form since Rei didn't want to "disappear from the minds of others" (EoTV quote) upon returning to Lilith, and it turns out, she didn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Jul 15, 2009 4:36 pm I liked Rei from the first time I saw her since shes the character who just does not give a shit what happens to her as long as she can complete her mission. The more the show went on the more that grew.

[]:             Quote Post by NickFury90 » Wed Jul 15, 2009 4:48 pm Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: I liked Rei from the first time I saw her since shes the character who just does not give a shit what happens to her as long as she can complete her mission. The more the show went on the more that grew.

[]:             Quote Post by TheWaffleCult » Wed Jul 22, 2009 8:40 pm I really like this first shot of her because it's a little vague as to whether or not Shinji saw her. The sudden cut to the birds and whatnot; it was a nice way to give a creepy impression of Rei's importance before the whole "cute injured girl pilot" scene. Basically, I ignored Rei's actual presence in this scene and accepted it as a nice piece of storytelling.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Thu Jul 23, 2009 6:22 am I think it's like "bad wolf" from doctor who. once Rei is all powerful putting images (possibley in Shinji's memory) is a cinch.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:07 am Hmm... somehow missed this earlier. First, forget the alternate reality stuff... that was just conjecture anyway. The point I was trying to make is that I don't think it's correct to call it a loop because future events aren't influencing or causing the past. 'Phantom' Rei doesn't seem to do anything at all that changes the outcome of events, she simply observes or asks questions, probably to satisfy her own curiosity about how things got this way. Yes, I'd say the bookends are the same version of Rei just watching things happen. I think that Rei managed to step outside of time in the process of ending 3I, and thus is free to observe it in all it's detail, past, present and future, at her leisure. She probably watches Shinji's entire life, but he only manages to notice her in those two moments.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 23, 2009 10:56 am NemZ wrote: Yes, I'd say the bookends are the same version of Rei just watching things happen. I think that Rei managed to step outside of time in the process of ending 3I, and thus is free to observe it in all it's detail, past, present and future, at her leisure. She probably watches Shinji's entire life, but he only manages to notice her in those two moments.
